name: Split supersymmetry
summary: Arkani-Hamed and Dimopoulos (2004) proposed abandoning the naturalness motivation for low-scale SUSY in favor of a heavy scalar sector (squarks, sleptons, heavy Higgses at 10^4 - 10^{16} GeV) while keeping fermionic superpartners (gauginos, Higgsinos) at the TeV scale, where they preserve gauge-coupling unification and provide a dark-matter candidate. Accepts cosmological-constant-like fine-tuning of the Higgs mass while retaining SUSY's other successes. Mini-split variants (Arvanitaki et al. 2012, arXiv:1210.0555) put scalars at 10^2-10^4 TeV (PeV scale), motivated by anomaly-mediation and consistent with the 125 GeV Higgs.

addresses_puzzles.note: TeV neutralino (Higgsino or wino LSP) is thermal relic with correct abundance for Higgsino mass ~ 1 TeV or wino mass ~ 2.9 TeV. Direct detection via Higgs/Z exchange suppressed in split-SUSY by mixing through heavy scalar sector; current LZ bounds compatible.

Gauginos and Higgsinos at TeV scale preserve precise gauge-coupling unification at M_GUT ~ 2 x 10^16 GeV, just as in MSSM. Heavy scalars do not spoil this since they appear in complete SU(5) multiplets above their decoupling scale.
Heavy stops above ~10 TeV stabilise the Higgs quartic from rolling to negative values, eliminating SM vacuum-instability concerns through one-loop matching at the heavy-scalar scale.

Explicitly abandons naturalness for the Higgs mass - relies on anthropic / landscape selection for the small electroweak scale. Trades hierarchy-naturalness fine-tuning for CC-like-tuning logic. Not all theorists accept this as 'addressing' the hierarchy problem.
Heavy scalars decoupled but light gauginos/higgsinos contribute via T and S. Compatible with CDF for tan(beta) ~ 1 and TeV-scale chargino masses.

touches_benchmarks.note: Heavy scalars decouple; Higgs couplings essentially SM. Below current sensitivity.

Higgsino DM: sigma_SI ~ 10^-48 cm^2 (Higgs-exchange-loop suppressed), approaching LZ sensitivity; wino DM: sigma_SI ~ 10^-47 cm^2 (Z-exchange), within ~factor 5 of current bound. Both within reach of next-generation XENONnT / DARWIN.

new_fields: gauginos (gluino, wino, bino) at TeV scale
introduces.note: Scalar mass scale m_S, gaugino mass parameters M_1, M_2, M_3, mu (Higgsino mass). Far fewer free parameters than MSSM since the entire scalar sector is degenerate at m_S; flavor and CP problems automatically suppressed by decoupling.

history: Arkani-Hamed and Dimopoulos (2004), 'Supersymmetric unification without low energy supersymmetry and signatures for fine-tuning at the LHC', JHEP 06, 073, arXiv:hep-th/0405159. Companion paper: Giudice-Romanino (2004), 'Split supersymmetry', Nucl. Phys. B 699, 65, arXiv:hep-ph/0406088. Mini-split with predicted 125 GeV Higgs from PeV scalars: Arvanitaki, Craig, Dimopoulos, Villadoro (2012), 'Mini-split', JHEP 02, 126, arXiv:1210.0555. Post-LHC analyses: Hall-Nomura (2014), Acharya et al. (2016).

references.citation: Arkani-Hamed and Dimopoulos, 'Supersymmetric unification without low energy supersymmetry and signatures for fine-tuning at the LHC', JHEP 06, 073 (2005)
Giudice and Romanino, 'Split supersymmetry', Nucl. Phys. B 699, 65 (2004)
